title: hypericum tomentosum subsp. lusitanicum
intro: Hypericum tomentosum subsp. lusitanicum is a subspecies of the St. John's Wort plant, belonging to the Hypericaceae family, characterized by its hairy stems and yellow flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Hypericum tomentosum subsp. lusitanicum include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, as well as being dried or made into tinctures for further use.
